sources: https://battlebots.fandom.com/api.php (MediaWiki API, per-robot 'Bot' infoboxes and per-team 'Team' infoboxes)
https://battlebots.com/world-championship-vii-robots/ (WC VII robot cards: rosters + team photos)
https://battlebots.com/robot/<slug>-wcvii/ (per-robot detail pages for DOOM, Doomba, Double Tap, Dragon King)
https://battlebots.fandom.com/api.php pageimages thumbnails (photo fallback for bots without a battlebots.com team photo)
notes: Active roster = the 24 competitors in Groups A-F of the 2026 'BattleBots Pro League powered by Bright Data' (premiered July 2, 2026 on YouTube after a delay from the announced June 25 date), as listed on the BattleBots fan wiki Pro League page; battlebots.com/proleague confirms 24 robots but publishes no roster table. Claw Viper, Hydra, Deep-Sea Shark, Whiplash and SawBlaze were invited/announced but withdrew or declined and were replaced by Disarray, Switchback, Ribbot and Bloodsport; Calypso, Banshee and Nemesis are 2026 alternates (active=false). Hometowns, teams, weapons, seasons and captains come from battlebots.fandom.com infoboxes; where a team relocated, the first-listed (original) hometown is used. Season labels: WC I-VII are the 2015-2023 reboot seasons; Comedy Central seasons 1.0-5.0 are 2000-2002; spinoffs (Bounty Hunters, Champions I/II, Proving Ground, FaceOffs) listed where the wiki records them. Historical coverage is a curated ~100-bot subset of well-known competitors, not the complete all-time field. Enrichment (2026-07-12): added 4 WC VII competitors missing from the seed (DOOM, Doomba, Double Tap, Dragon King; details from their battlebots.com WCVII robot pages) and added 'photo'/'photo_source' to every entry. photo_source 'battlebots.com' = official team photo from the WC VII or earlier season roster pages (full-size original URLs verified HTTP 200; Bloodsport's WC VII card image is mislabeled on the site, so the correctly named BB2022-bloodsport-team.jpg is used); 'fandom' = wiki pageimages thumbnail (usually a robot photo, not a team photo). Name variants resolved: 'Rusty Jr.' (WC VII card) = seed 'Rusty'; 'SMEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE' = 'SMEE'; 'Obwalden Overlord' = 'The Obwalden Overlord'; 'ATOM#94' = 'ATOM 94'; 'WanHoo' = 'Wan Hoo'; fandom 'Nemesis (2026)' used for the USC bot (plain 'Nemesis' is an unrelated 2001 heavyweight).
